auth_type: API Key or OAuth2 Client Credentials
auth_notes: Most internal communication systems require a Bearer Token or a custom X-API-Key header. Enterprise-grade internal tools often require OAuth2 Client Credentials flow involving a Client ID and Client Secret to generate a temporary access token.
rate_limits: Typically 60-100 requests per minute for internal enterprise APIs, though this varies significantly based on the specific internal infrastructure.
gotchas: Internal APIs often reside behind a VPN or Firewall, requiring n8n to be self-hosted or using a static IP/Tunnel.
Payload structures for custom internal tools are often poorly documented compared to public SaaS.
SSL certificate issues if the internal API uses self-signed certificates.
client_must_provide: Authentication Credentials (API Key or OAuth2 details)
API Documentation or JSON Schema for endpoints
research_notes: Since this refers to a non-native internal system, the integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. You will need to manually configure the Method (POST/GET), Headers (Authorization), and Body (JSON). If the system supports outgoing webhooks, use the n8n Webhook node to receive data in real-time.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Since this refers to a non-native internal system, the integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. You will need to manually configure the Method (POST/GET), Headers (Authorization), and Body (JSON). If the system supports outgoing webhooks, use the n8n Webhook node to receive data in real-time.
